name: Vía Verde de Ojos Negros & Camino del Cid — From Sagunto to Segorbe
name_original: Vía Verde de Ojos Negros & Camino del Cid — De Sagunto a Segorbe
region_intro: Touring cycling around Sagunt / Sagunto offers a diverse landscape for riders. The region features coastal routes along the Mediterranean Sea, agricultural areas with citrus orchards, and historical greenways like the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros. Terrain varies from flat coastal paths to more challenging routes with moderate elevation gains, particularly inland. The area provides a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces suitable for touring bicycles. Best touring cycling routes around Sagunt / Sagunto The most popular touring cycling route is Vía Verde de Ojos Negros & Camino del Cid — From Sagunto to Segorbe, a 46.7 miles (75.1 km) trail that takes about 5 hours 20 minutes to complete. This route follows a historic greenway, connecting Sagunto with Segorbe through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Ojos Negros Greenway – Jérica loop from Sagunt, a difficult 58.9 miles (94.7 km) path. This loop explores sections of the Ojos Negros Greenway, offering views of the surrounding countryside. Local touring cyclists also love the Playa de Canet d'en Berenguer – Citrus Orchards loop from Canet d'en Berenguer, a 27.7 miles (44.6 km) trail leading through coastal areas and agricultural lands, often completed in about 2 hours 32 minutes. Touring cycling around Sagunt / Sagunto is defined by coastal paths, historical greenways, and agricultural landscapes. The network offers options for easy touring bike routes as well as more challenging bike paths near Sagunto. The routes in Sagunt / Sagunto are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 2000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Sagunt / Sagunto's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Sagunt / Sagunto?
A: There are over 80 touring cycling routes around Sagunt / Sagunto, offering a wide variety of experiences for cyclists of all levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Sagunt / Sagunto offers over 30 easy touring cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10379689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Canet d'en Berenguer – Path between two canals loop from el Port de Sagunt&lt;/a&gt; is a great option, covering 34.2 km with minimal elevation gain, perfect for a relaxed ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Sagunt / Sagunto?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape, from flat coastal paths along the Mediterranean Sea to agricultural areas with citrus orchards. You'll also find historical greenways like the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros. Terrain varies from paved surfaces to unpaved sections, with some inland routes featuring moderate elevation gains.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, Sagunt / Sagunto has several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e918419688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de Ojos Negros & Camino del Cid — From Sagunto to Segorbe&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 75.1 km route that follows a historic greenway, connecting Sagunto with Segorbe through varied landscapes.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Sagunt / Sagunto?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in the region are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31778417" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Canet d'en Berenguer – Citrus Orchards loop from Canet d'en Berenguer&lt;/a&gt;, a 44.6 km ride through coastal and agricultural lands.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes around Sagunt / Sagunto?
A: For experienced cyclists looking for a challenge, there are over 30 difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33844177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos Negros Greenway – Jérica loop from Sagunt&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 94.7 km path with significant elevation, exploring sections of the historic greenway and offering expansive countryside views.

Q: What can I see along the touring cycling routes in Sagunt / Sagunto?
A: Beyond the scenic landscapes, you can explore various natural attractions. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3046950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marjal dels Moros&lt;/a&gt; wetland, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4431242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ajuez Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2785413" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PortaCeli Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also pass through charming villages and historical sites.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Sagunt / Sagunto?
A: The Mediterranean climate makes Sagunt / Sagunto suitable for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and less crowded routes. Summer can be hot, especially inland, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winter is generally mild, making it a good option for those looking to escape colder climates.

Q: Are there routes that pass by interesting viewpoints?
A: Yes, many routes offer opportunities for scenic views. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2679470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Taiola, La Marjal d'Almenara&lt;/a&gt; is a notable viewpoint where you can observe the natural marshland. Routes often traverse elevated sections or coastal paths providing panoramic vistas of the sea and surrounding countryside.

Q: Are there any routes that explore historical sites or landmarks?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. While specific routes may not be dedicated solely to historical sites, many pass through areas with significant heritage. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14840687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Large mural – Torres Torres Castle loop from Les Valls&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the impressive Torres Torres Castle, offering a glimpse into the area's past.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Sagunt / Sagunto?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from coastal paths to greenways, and the variety of routes suitable for different skill levels. The well-maintained Vía Verde de Ojos Negros is frequently highlighted as a favorite.
tips: This beautiful castle was declared a National Monument in 1931, although it has been there for more than two thousand years and preserves something from each of the different cultures that settled and took possession of this strategic place. Its interior can be visited and there you will find innumerable historical treasures from the Iberian, Roman, medieval and modern times. In addition to its deep-rooted history, this site offers formidable views of the entire region and the Mediterranean.

One of the most recognized Greenways in all of Spain with a total length of more than 120 kilometers linking Teruel with Sagunto. Along it you can enjoy varied landscapes and pass through beautiful towns in the interior of Aragon and the Valencian Community. In terms of surface, you will find sections of pavement and others will be compacted gravel track. You will go through tunnels that require lights so remember to have them ready.
And as for the slope, from Teruel to Sagunto it is 90% downhill so you will be very comfortable, conversely you will have to overcome a slope of over 1000 metres.
No matter how you decide to go through it, you will still have a unique experience.

Segorbe is a very charming town, of great cultural, historical and scenic interest with very colorful and picturesque buildings. It is advisable to get lost in the streets and walk through it, since in every corner there is something curious or striking and it has many buildings of great historical importance such as the Cathedral.
